normalized_title: Six-month stolen-bike recovery with Rob Porter
published_title_visible: 13. It took about 6 months to get Harry Leary’s stolen BMX back - they knew where it was - Rob Porter
published_title_evidence_status: transcribed from supplied YouTube Studio screenshot; an ellipsis marks visible UI truncation where present
working_source_title: Fireside BMX Chat w/ Greg Mathias - It took about 6 months to get the Harry Leary’s stolen loaner Daylight BMX back
working_source_description: It took about 6 months to get the Harry Leary’s stolen loaner Daylight BMX back. There were jurisdiction issues. Rob Porter helped because the guy who was selling the bike lived near him. After multiple purchase attempts, the bike was finally recovered after the thief finally agreed to meet. The bike was returned to Daylight BMX and one of Richard’s daughters is now riding it. It still has the magic mushroom decals on it.
frame_mapping_basis: chronological order of the 32 supplied published-frame captures, aligned to the Studio register that omits 10 and contains two distinct records numbered 12
transcript_status: No standalone Short transcript was supplied. The timestamp range is a provisional access reference into the supplied parent machine transcript and requires audiovisual verification.
medical_or_sensitive_claim_qualification: The theft, jurisdiction, recovery, and later use of the bicycle are interview recollections unless separately verified.
rights: User-supplied Lititz BMX publication evidence. No open license implied.
